items.title: Author Correction: HLA polymorphisms shape divergent outcomes of Toxoplasma and Plasmodium infection in Eastern Indian HbE/β-thalassemia cohort
items.author: Budhaditya Mukherjee
items.content: Author Correction: HLA polymorphisms shape divergent outcomes of  and  infection in Eastern Indian HbE/β-thalassemia cohort

items.title: Thermosensitivity of cellular translation restricts the growth of fission yeast at high temperatures
items.author: Yuichi Morozumi
items.content: Genetic analysis of the Dri1–Rhs1 complex sheds light on the link between the thermosensitivity of protein translation and the upper temperature limit for cell growth in fission yeast.

items.title: Non-duality in brain and experience of advanced meditators—key role for intrinsic neural timescales
items.author: Georg Northoff
items.content: Advanced meditators show similar duration of intrinsic neural timescales during internal and external attention, suggesting a neural signature of non-dual awareness.

items.title: Predatory myxobacteria lay at the cross-roads of metallo-β-lactamase evolution
items.author: Stathis D. Kotsakis
items.content: Phylogenomic and functional analyses suggest that predatory myxobacteria had a key role in the early evolution of B1 metallo-β-lactamases and revealed the adaptations required for expression of resistance in enterobacterial pathogens.

items.title: Multi-drug tolerance in Leishmania persister-like cells
items.author: Malgorzata A. Domagalska
items.content: Leishmania persister-like cells survive lethal doses of antimonials by adopting a quiescent state, they demonstrate cross-tolerance to other anti-leishmanial drugs and become hyper-resistant in the presence of pre-existing drug resistance.

items.title: Cx43 levels guide apicobasal polarity in regenerating airway epithelial cells
items.author: Marc Chanson
items.content: Mechanistic dissection of airway epithelium repair shows how aberrant Cx43 stabilization alters cell orientation, secretory axis structure, and fibronectin secretion, leading to barrier defects and increased P. aeruginosa adherence in cystic fibrosis.

items.title: Porphyromonas gingivalis promotes the malignant phenotype of oral squamous cell carcinoma through the recruitment of Treg cells
items.author: Bin Ling
items.content: Histological and P. gingivalis-infected OSCC models elucidate that it fosters an immunosuppressive microenvironment by promoting CCL20 secretion to recruit CCR6⁺ regulatory T cells.

items.title: Author Correction: Label-free human skin imaging with enhanced molecular contrast via time-resolved fluorescence and advanced phasor analysis
items.author: Mihaela Balu
items.content: Author Correction: Label-free human skin imaging with enhanced molecular contrast via time-resolved fluorescence and advanced phasor analysis
